titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in 423
GA 423
The New Testament
Das Neue Testament
The Gospel according to John
john

respStmt.name: the International Greek New Testament Project

titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the International Greek New Testament Project in its work towards the Editio Critica Maior of John between 2010 and 2013.

editorialDecl.p: The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE, later converted to XML. The original transcription guidelines can be found at
The plain text file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.4 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Some commentary text removed from 5.33 by Rachel Kevern.
παιδιον changed to *παιδιων C παιδιον at 16.21 by Rachel Kevern.
υμετερω changed to ημετερω at 8.17; απαρνηση/ει changed to απαρνησ[1-2] at 13.38; προσφερειν changed to προφερειν at 16.2 by Rachel Kevern.
ελεγεν changed to ελεγε at 2.22 and και changed to το at 5.27 by Rachel Kevern.
21.12 removed, and φησι removed from 21.25 and editorial note added by Rachel Kevern.
μετ deleted at 13.18 by Rachel Kevern.
κατεαξαν changed to κατεαξαν at 19.33 by Rachel Kevern.
αυτω α changed to αυτω[1] at 12.47 by Rachel Kevern.
Word break in 13.17 corrected by Rachel Kevern.
Text of 1.1 in commentary wrongly identified as lemma removed, word break corrected at 1.32 by David Parker.
Converted from plain text by Catherine Smith using Python conversion scripts 1.0.

body.note: Status:G-A Number:423München, Bayer, Staatsbibl.,Gr.36.37Name of book transcribed: JohnFirst folio transcribed: 1rectoBase text used: TRns Transcribed by: WJETranscription begun: 15.10.10Transcription finished: 26.11.10 Multiple line break and commentary tags added by AB on 20.03.12. A few problems highlighted by AB were fixed by RK on 22.03.12 RK found more problems relating to much text tagged as missing and asked SG to fix. SG fixed on 23.11.2012. Does this manuscript contain (delete and complete as appropriate): Lectionary annotations - No Initials and other enlarged letters - Yes Diples (>) - Yes , double throughout the manuscript denoting biblical passages Obeloi (†) - No Other remarks: Where words are broken across the line a "-" is added(sometimes unnecessarily);the two repeats on f203v and 204v are too good - they must be a photographer's mistake; somewhere in e-mail correspondence this MS is described as a catena. That is not so. Blocks of Biblical text are followed by blocks of commentary. The scribe uses a device like a flying seagull for ων (eg f390, v38, whereas it could be construed as a simple ω. Folio numbers have been changed oveer a long period - in most cases I have quoted both numbers.Note by RK: I agree with Bill that 203v and 204v are photographic duplications, so I assume that the ‘real’ 204v did not get photographed. The text has been transcribed twice here, but wasn’t written twice by the scribe. There is no scan of F336v so Alan was unable to work out what was going on in Bill’s transcription of this page. The scan of folio 345r was not available to either Bill or Alan.A number of verses were put in parentheses. I assume that Bill means they were omitted by the scribe so for now I’ve put them in curly brackets. These are being fixed by Sam.

ab.note: Correction is in scribendo. I think a line was added to the α in καμ to try to make it look like ει. This was then abandoned and the whole word struck out. -- MAC
9.3b is repeated and marked as biblical text: αλλ ινα φανερωθη τα εργα του θυ̅ εν αυτω
There is no scan available of this page.

ab.note: There is no scan available of this page.
The omega may have been written twice, the first time less clearly.
This was marked out so the lemma could start on a new line--MAC
